title: Ctenopteris flabelliformis
intro: Ctenopteris flabelliformis, also known as the fan fern, is a species of flowering plant in the family Polypodiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and aiding in the treatment of fever and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Ctenopteris flabelliformis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and polysaccharides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Ctenopteris flabelliformis is often used in traditional Chinese medicine and can be found in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and dried extracts, which are used to promote overall well-being and support immune function.
taxonomy: Ctenopteris flabelliformis is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It belongs to the order Polypodiales. This species is further classified into the genus Ctenopteris. The classification of Ctenopteris flabelliformis is based on its morphological characteristics and molecular studies. The exact classification may vary depending on the source and the most recent taxonomic updates.
